scheme_name: Training/Capacity Building Programmes/Research work on Remote Sensing and GIS Applications
page_title: Training/Capacity Building Programmes/Research work on Remote Sensing and GIS Applications

details: The scheme “Training/Capacity Building Programmes/Research work on Remote Sensing and GIS Applications” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Space Application Centre (USAC) under the Science and Technology, Government of Uttarakhand. It aims to strengthen scientific and technical capacity of researchers, students, and government personnel in the field of Remote Sensing and Geographic Information System (GIS) applications by providing training, conducting capacity-building programmes, and supporting research initiatives.
benefits: Researchers are selected for scientific research projects funded by the Central and State Governments and trained in Remote Sensing and GIS applications. The training is provided free of cost. Selected researchers (JRFs/SRFs/Project Scientists) receive stipends (including HRA) as per the regulations of the respective funding organizations. Researchers are required to make their own arrangements for food and accommodation.

The program also offers training in Remote Sensing and GIS applications to postgraduate students in their final semester/year of study at various universities across the state, as well as to undergraduate students through internships. The training is free of cost, and students are expected to arrange their own food and accommodation during the training period.

Capacity-building programs in Remote Sensing and GIS are organized periodically for various line departments based on their requirements. No participation fees are charged from these departments. Boarding and lodging during these programs are arranged by the concerned departments with the support of U-SAC.

Workshops on Geographic Information Systems are also organized periodically in degree colleges and schools across the state, as per requests and requirements, to motivate students towards space technology. U-SAC provides free one-day training sessions along with complimentary meals and transportation; however, accommodation is not provided.
eligibility: The following are eligible to participate in the Training and Capacity Building Programme:
Students who have passed a Postgraduate degree (preferably in the Science stream) with First Division from any recognized university in India.
Final semester/year postgraduate students (preferably from the Science stream) of any recognized university (private, state, or central) in India.
Personnel from the Central Government and various line departments of the State Government.
Graduate and higher-level students from government, private, and central universities or colleges within the state.

offline: If required for a project, information is provided on the U-SAC portal (

), and selection is made through a walk-in interview within the stipulated period. For this purpose, students are required to submit their postgraduate (first-class) certificates and other academic documents, experience certificates, training certificates, Aadhaar card, bank account details, and caste certificate. Upon successfully qualifying in the interview, the researchers are awarded a research fellowship.

The Vice-Chancellor/Dean/Registrar/Head of Department of the concerned University sends a request letter to the Director, U-SAC, on behalf of the final semester/year postgraduate students (mainly from the science stream) for dissertation/internship at the Centre. Based on this request, and upon receiving the recommendation from the Director, U-SAC, the students are invited for training. For registration, the trainees are required to submit all necessary documents such as Aadhaar card, educational certificates, and mobile number.

Departmental personnel may send a request letter to the Director, U-SAC, through their respective Head of Department. U-SAC organizes capacity-building programs for line departments from time to time, in which officers and personnel of various levels, authorized by their Head of Department, participate. In addition, training programs are also organized from time to time on the request of line departments as per their requirements. There is no provision for training for any specific individual.

The Principal, Head of Department, or Dean of an institute or degree college may send a request letter to the Director, U-SAC. After determining the duration of the training, U-SAC informs the concerned institution, which may then bring its students for the visit.

documents_required: Educational Certificates and Other Academic Documents

faqs.question: Who are eligible for research training under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Researchers who have passed Postgraduate (preferably Science stream) in first division from a recognized Indian university are eligible. This includes JRF/SRF/Project Scientists selected for government-funded projects.

faqs.question: Are students eligible for training?
faqs.answer: Yes. Final semester/year postgraduate students and undergraduate students through internships from recognized universities in India can participate.

faqs.question: Is there any fee for the training programs?
faqs.answer: No. All training programs, capacity-building programmes, and workshops are free of cost for students, researchers, and departmental personnel.

faqs.question: Are food and accommodation provided?
faqs.answer: Researchers and students need to make their own arrangements for food and accommodation. For departmental training programs, boarding and lodging are provided by the respective line department with U-SAC support. For workshops in colleges/schools, free meals and transportation are provided, but accommodation is not provided.

faqs.question: Who can participate in departmental capacity-building programs?
faqs.answer: Personnel from central and state government departments are eligible. Participation is authorized through the Head of Department. Training is organized based on departmental requirements.

faqs.question: How are participants selected for research projects?
faqs.answer: Selection is made through a walk-in interview within a stipulated period. Researchers must submit their postgraduate certificates, academic documents, experience certificates, training certificates, Aadhaar card, bank account, and caste certificate.

faqs.question: How can students register for training or internships?
faqs.answer: The Vice Chancellor/Dean/Registrar/Head of the University Department sends a request letter to the Director of U-SAC on behalf of students. Based on U-SAC’s recommendation, students are called for training and must submit relevant documents for registration.

faqs.question: How are research projects funded under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Research projects are funded by central and state government organizations. U-SAC provides training and support for researchers working on these projects.

faqs.question: Where can more information about the scheme be found?
faqs.answer: Information is available on the U-SAC portal: www.u-sac.in , which includes notifications, eligibility details, and selection procedures.
